assertions.reason: Tuistory did not successfully dispatch Ctrl+7 in this session (known chord limitation), so the keyboard shortcut could not be fully validated through the live surface; tab activation was corroborated via click flow and passing unit test coverage.
GitHub notifications render title, repository, reason, and human-readable timestamp in the list/detail view.
PullRequest notifications are visually distinguished with the PR icon (🔀) versus regular notification icon (🔔).
Single-notification mark-read worked from the GitHub tab; selected notification became stale/removed from unread list, and API read endpoint returned success.

Shift+R keypath could not be conclusively triggered via tuistory in this run (no observable UI transition after multiple attempts). Endpoint behavior and action wiring are corroborated by curl + pytest, but live shortcut dispatch remains unverified.
Pressing 'o' on a selected PR notification produced in-app opened-browser feedback, and code/test corroboration confirms webbrowser.open(url) is called with the notification URL.
Unread badge/count was visible as GitHub (50) and updated to no badge with 0 notifications after read-all propagation and refresh.
With unread notifications reduced to zero, GitHub tab displayed the expected empty state message ('All clear! 🔔').

frictions: tuistory did not dispatch Ctrl+7 reliably in this session; used click fallback and pytest corroboration for keyboard binding behavior.
tuistory could not conclusively trigger Shift+R (mark-all-read) despite repeated attempts; used curl + pytest corroboration.
GitHub read-all API effect was eventually consistent: unread count remained 50 immediately after POST /read-all, then dropped to 0 after ~5 seconds.
blockers: VAL-GH-001 and VAL-GH-005 are blocked for full live keyboard-path verification due tuistory key-chord/input limitations in this run.
notes: Validated on localhost/127.0.0.1:9849 only. Tuistory session was closed after evidence capture.
